title: CF Door Data Contracts
source_policy: Derived from the byte-equivalent CF source scaffold, the decoded CF sector-list contract, and C0 movement/interaction consumers. This promotes only the type 0, type 2, and type 6 payload shapes directly read by those consumers.

type0_event_gated_script_pointer.consumer: C0:6A3F masks 0x7FFF for C2:1628 and bit 15 supplies the expected flag state before queueing
C0:6A69 reads this low word and enqueues it as queue type 0
C0:6A6D reads this bank word for the queue payload consumed by C0:75DD/C10004

type2_door_transition_record.consumer: C0:6C22 reads the script low word from the queued CF pointer before optional C10004 dispatch
C0:6C1F reads the script bank word from the queued CF pointer before optional C10004 dispatch
C0:6C6B treats zero as no gate; otherwise it tests 0x7FFF through C2:1628 and bit 15 as expected state
C0:6D0A masks this word with 0x3FFF, scales by 8, passes it as the Y-side position, and also uses the raw word for placement direction lookup
C0:6CFE scales this word by 8 and passes it as the X-side position to transition/context refresh helpers
C0:6CCB/C0:6DBF read this byte to select the D0:1400 screen-transition config and associated SFX/effect handling

type6_cached_interaction_pointer.consumer: C0:663F reads this low word from the type-6 CF payload and stores it in $5DDE
C0:663C reads this bank word from the type-6 CF payload and stores it in $5DE0
